expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: Transcript 'Starting state (baseline)' acknowledges both kelet.configure() and kelet.agentic_session() are already present. Decisions section adds new signals on top without re-implementing the basics. changes.diff does not show configure() or agentic_session() being added — only new signals and the feedback endpoint.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md documents 6 signals: user_id threading to agentic_session, rate-limit signal, agent error signal, explicit user feedback endpoint, search no-results signal, and get-page not-found signal. All implemented in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than just Task Completion
expectations.evidence: No deeplink.txt in without_skill outputs. Transcript has no mention of synthetic evaluators or the Kelet console deeplink feature at all. Without the skill, there is no awareness of the synthetic evaluator setup workflow.

expectations.text: Does not propose VoteFeedback or React since app has no React frontend
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md explicitly states: 'No React component possible (plain HTML), so a REST endpoint allows a simple native thumbs widget in the existing HTML frontend.' No VoteFeedback or React code in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md has a 'Rationale:' section for each signal explaining the value (e.g. rate-limit: 'Rate-limit hits are a failure signal — they indicate frustrated users who are trying to use the product but being blocked'; search no-results: 'High no-results rate = docs don't cover what users ask'). Each entry explains the observable failure category.
